sections.title: Test Scores
sections.blurb: Where you stand on standardized tests.

sections.title: Preferences
sections.blurb: The kind of school you want.

questions.text: It's a free Saturday afternoon. Which of these sounds most fun?

options.label: Building a small app or automating something on my computer
Sketching, painting, or editing a video project
Reading about history, philosophy, or current events
Watching science documentaries or doing a hands-on experiment

questions.text: Would you rather debug a computer program or design a poster?

options.label: Debug the program — I love figuring out why things break
Design the poster — visuals and layout excite me
Honestly, either one sounds fun
Neither — I'd rather work with people than pixels

questions.text: How do you feel about mathematics?

options.label: I love it — proofs and problem sets are genuinely satisfying
I like it as a tool for solving real-world problems
I can do it, but I wouldn't choose it
I avoid it whenever possible

questions.text: A new AI model just made headlines. What's your reaction?

options.label: I want to understand how it works under the hood
I wonder what it means for jobs, ethics, and society
I think about the business opportunities it creates
Mild curiosity, but it's not really my thing

questions.text: Which class discussion would you most eagerly join?

options.label: Whether free will exists
How the brain forms memories
Why some economies grow and others stagnate
Whether we could colonize Mars

questions.text: How much does helping sick or struggling people directly appeal to you as a career?

options.label: It's my main motivation — medicine or healthcare is the goal
I care about health, but more at the systems and policy level
I'd rather help people in other ways, like teaching or advocacy
Not a major career driver for me

questions.text: You're handed a broken gadget. What do you do?

options.label: Take it apart immediately — I need to see how it works
Look up a repair guide and follow it carefully
Find someone who can fix it and watch them do it
Replace it — my time is better spent elsewhere

questions.text: Which headline would you click first?

options.label: "New exoplanet discovered in habitable zone"
"Gene therapy cures rare childhood disease"
"Startup reaches $1B valuation in 18 months"
"Lost novel by famous author discovered"

questions.text: How do you feel about writing essays?

options.label: I love it — crafting an argument or story is my strength
I'm good at it, though I prefer analytical writing over creative
It's fine, but I'd rather show my work with numbers or code
I strongly prefer projects and problem sets to essays

questions.text: Climate change comes up in conversation. What's your angle?

options.label: The science — emissions, feedback loops, climate modeling
The technology — renewables, batteries, carbon capture
The policy — carbon taxes, treaties, and incentives
It matters, but it's not what I want to work on

questions.text: Which museum would you pick on a city trip?

options.label: An art museum
A natural history or science museum
A history or cultural museum
Skip the museum — I'd rather explore neighborhoods and food

questions.text: How interested are you in learning foreign languages?

options.label: Very — I actively study one or more languages for fun
Somewhat — I enjoy it as a window into other cultures
Only as much as school requires
I'm more interested in programming languages

questions.text: Your friend group is starting a small business. Which role do you claim?

options.label: CEO — strategy, pitching, and closing deals
CFO — pricing, margins, and the financial model
CTO — building the product
Creative director — brand, design, and storytelling

questions.text: What kind of problem is most satisfying to solve?

options.label: One with a single provably correct answer
One where I build something people actually use
One where I change someone's mind or behavior
One with no right answer, where interpretation matters

questions.text: Which lab or workspace sounds most like home?

options.label: A wet lab with pipettes, cultures, and microscopes
A machine shop or robotics lab
A studio with easels, instruments, or a stage
A quiet library carrel stacked with books

questions.text: How do you feel about public speaking and debate?

options.label: I thrive on it — persuading a room is a rush
I'm comfortable when I know the material deeply
I prefer writing my arguments instead
I'd rather let my work speak for itself

questions.text: Pick the elective you'd take just for fun.

options.label: Psychology of decision-making
Astronomy: from black holes to the Big Bang
Creative writing workshop
Intro to game development

questions.text: A documentary about global cultures and migration is on. Do you watch?

options.label: Absolutely — how people live around the world fascinates me
Yes, especially for the history and geopolitics
Maybe, if friends are watching
I'd switch to something about nature or technology

questions.text: Where do you land on working with data?

options.label: I love finding patterns in messy datasets
Useful for answering questions about people and markets
I like data in science — measurements, experiments, models
Data leaves me cold; I prefer qualitative depth

questions.text: Twenty years from now, which achievement would make you proudest?

options.label: A discovery or invention that pushed human knowledge forward
A company or product used by millions
Art, writing, or performances that moved people
Policy or advocacy that made society fairer or healthier

questions.text: Which subject do you consistently earn your best grades in?

options.label: Math
Science (biology, chemistry, or physics)
English or history
It's pretty even across the board

questions.text: What's your unweighted GPA (on a 4.0 scale)?

options.label: Below 3.4

questions.text: How many AP, IB, or honors courses will you finish by graduation?

options.label: 10 or more

questions.text: Which advanced course did you (or would you) most enjoy?

options.label: AP Calculus BC or AP Statistics
AP Biology or AP Chemistry
AP Literature or AP World History
AP Computer Science or AP Physics

questions.text: How would teachers describe you as a student?

options.label: Top of the class — I push for the hardest material available
Strong and consistent, with occasional standout work
Capable, but I focus my effort on subjects I care about
Still finding my academic footing

questions.text: When a class gets genuinely difficult, what do you do?

options.label: Grind through it — hard material is why I'm here
Get help early from teachers, tutors, or classmates
Do enough to keep my grade safe
Honestly, I tend to disengage

questions.text: Have you done any independent academic work beyond class?

options.label: Yes — research with a lab, professor, or science fair project
Yes — self-taught skills like coding, a language, or an instrument
Some — summer programs or online courses
Not yet

questions.text: Which describes your ideal college workload?

options.label: Deep and technical — problem sets, labs, and rigor
Reading- and discussion-heavy seminars
Project- and portfolio-based creation
A balanced mix of everything

questions.text: What's your SAT score (or converted ACT)?

options.label: Below 1300 / haven't taken it yet

questions.text: Which SAT section is stronger for you?

options.label: Math, by a clear margin
Reading and writing, by a clear margin
They're about even
Haven't taken it yet

questions.text: How do you perform on high-stakes tests in general?

options.label: I usually outperform my practice scores
I perform right around my preparation level
Nerves sometimes cost me points
Tests really don't reflect my ability

questions.text: Which extracurricular takes up most of your time?

options.label: STEM clubs — robotics, math team, coding, science olympiad
Arts — band, theater, choir, studio art, film
Leadership and service — student government, volunteering, debate
Publications — newspaper, yearbook, literary magazine

questions.text: How deep does your main activity go?

options.label: State/national level recognition or a leadership title I earned
Multiple years of committed involvement
Regular participation, nothing formal
I spread my time across many casual activities

questions.text: Have you ever volunteered in a hospital, clinic, or care setting?

options.label: Yes, extensively — it confirmed my interest in healthcare
A little, and I'd like to do more
No, but I volunteer in other community settings
No, that's not my scene

questions.text: Have you ever made money from your own venture (tutoring, resale, an app, art commissions)?

options.label: Yes — I've run something real with actual customers
Small experiments here and there
No, but I'd love to try
No, entrepreneurship doesn't interest me

questions.text: Outside school, what do you create or maintain?

options.label: Code — personal projects, games, or open source
Creative work — music, art, videos, or stories
A garden, animals, collections, or nature projects
Mostly my grades and my friendships

questions.text: What campus size feels right?

options.label: Small and tight-knit (under 5,000 undergrads)
Big and buzzing (15,000+)
Size doesn't matter to me

questions.text: Where do you want to spend the next four years?

options.label: The heart of a big city
A classic college town
A leafy suburban campus
No strong preference

questions.text: How do you feel about applying to ultra-selective schools?

options.label: Bring it on — I want the most selective program I can reach
I'll apply to some reaches alongside realistic options
I'd rather target schools where I'm clearly competitive
Selectivity doesn't factor into my choices

questions.text: Which matters most in your ideal program?

options.label: Flexibility to combine fields and design my own path
Depth — world-class training in one discipline
Career outcomes and professional networks
Hands-on making, performing, or research from day one
